title: Pamela S. Debias | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP

Pamela joined Fragomen in 2014 and is Counsel at Fragomen’s Detroit office. In this role, she partners with a wide range of corporate clients and their employees on various employment-based immigration matters, including immigrant and nonimmigrant visa processing, U.S. permanent residence, immigration policy and regulatory compliance matters. Pamela has extensive experience providing strategic direction to clients undergoing corporate restructures, collaborating with clients on short- and long-term retention of international talent and is a trusted advisor to business leaders, mobility management, HR and legal teams.
Prior to joining Fragomen in 2014, Pamela practiced in the area of corporate, employment and family-based immigration law. She assisted a diverse range of clients, including multinational corporations, small businesses and individuals.
Pamela is an active member in the legal community and is dedicated to various pro bono efforts, which include service with American Immigration Lawyers Association ’s Military Assistance Program (MAP) and Michigan Immigrant Rights Center . She also previously served on the Dilley Pro Bono Project, where she spent one week at the South Texas Family Residential Center in Dilley, Texas providing pro bono legal services to women and children.
Pamela speaks English and Maltese.
- University of Detroit Mercy School of Law, J.D., 2008
- Eastern Michigan University, B.S., cum laude , 2004
Bar Admissions: Michigan
Court Admissions: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Michigan
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Chambers USA , 2023 to 2025

### Professional accomplishments
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association (AILA)
Member, AILA Michigan Chapter
Member, State Bar of Michigan
Member, Michigan Relocation Council
Managed high volume clients in the automotive and manufacturing industries, as well as university clients.
Provided strategic advice on complex corporate restructures and the impact to employees and other compliance matters.
Oversaw and managed high volume immigration projects, including nonimmigrant and immigrant visa processing, PERM and National Interest Waivers.

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Need to Know: Immigration & Business Travel.” Global Business Travel Association – Michigan Chapter , Webinar, 19 August 2021.
Speaker, “Business Immigration in the Trump Administration.” American Society of Employers , Webinar, 18 August 2020.
Speaker, “Legal and Compliance Issues Around the COVID-19 Pandemic.” American Society of Employers , Webinar, 26 March 2020.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
